rulings.Case Title: OASIS GRILL, LLC ET AL VS. ERIC KETCHUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Oasis Grill
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 6, 2021 line 1. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Oasis Grill is continued to July 7, 2021 to afford the parties an opportunity to engage in a meaningful meet and confer process with each side eschewing rigidity and seeking to accommodate each other's reasonable concerns. The Court provides the following considerations to assist the parties in their efforts to reach a resolution of this motion. First, if Oasis wishes to eliminate or reduce duplication of its deposition with the deposition of Mr. Zughaiyer, Oasis must agree in writing that all deposition testimony of Mr. Zughaiyer is binding on Oasis as if that testimony were given as a PMK of Oasis. Second, if as expected Oasis so agrees, the parties should commit to the extent reasonably possible to avoid duplication of Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition. One possible way to do so is, after reviewing the transcript of Mr. Zughaiyer, the parties should discuss each of the 39 "matters for examination" to attempt to agree which were fully covered at Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition and which matters were either not covered at all or insufficiently covered. Third, the parties should understand that because they did not anticipate, much less resolve, the issue of duplication prior to or at the time of the taking of Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition, there likely will be some overlap of the two depositions and almost certainly follow-up questions to answers given at Mr. Zughaiyer's deposition. Fourth, the parties should agree to a mutually convenient date certain for the deposition of Oasis and, absent an emergency, the deponent and counsel for plaintiffs must appear. If the parties have reached a resolution of this motion, they need not appear at the hearing and the motion will be taken off calendar. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LIDIA J. DIAZ ET AL VS. IMELDA DIAZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 6, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT PRISCELLA BUGARIN MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is denied. Assuming without deciding that service of summons and complaint were made by means of a trespass, which is the sole argument asserted for this motion, the moving party cites no authority, nor is the court aware of any, that service accomplished by trespass invalidates the service. The moving party must file an answer or other response to the complaint within ten calendar days. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL GARDENS OF BETHEL A.M.A. CHURCH, INC. VS. ANTOINETTE COMMER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 6, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT ANTOINETTE COMMER, CHARLES COMMER DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is overruled. Because the complaint alleges sufficient information to enable defendants to file an answer to the claim for unlawful detainer, the complaint is not uncertain. If defendants desire further information, they may seek that information via discovery. Defendants must file an answer within five calendar days. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
